subject: Project Meridian — Data Mapping Document v1

messages.body:
Marcus, Sarah,

Attached is the data mapping document v1 — delivered on time as committed. 

Quick overview of what's in here:
- Full field-level mapping from HL7 v2 segments to FHIR R4 resources (Patient, Encounter, Observation, DiagnosticReport)
- Custom lab extensions: we've proposed handling the 15 custom fields as FHIR extensions using the http://acmehealth.org/fhir/StructureDefinition namespace. See Section 4. These will need your sign-off on the extension definitions.
- Known gaps: there are 3 fields in the ADT A08 segment (patient update event) where the source system behavior isn't fully documented. I've flagged these with [NEEDS_CLARIFICATION] tags. Linda — you may be best positioned to answer these.
- Transformation logic for null values, unknown codes, and legacy enum values that don't have FHIR equivalents is documented in Section 6.

I'd suggest Linda and Marcus focus your review on Sections 3 (ADT mappings) and 4 (custom extensions). The rest is fairly standard and I'd expect lower friction there.

Target: your review comments back to me by January 22nd so we can finalize before I build against this.

Let me know if you have questions.
Tom

[Attachment: Meridian_DataMapping_v1_20260115.xlsx — 847KB]

attachments.description: Data mapping spreadsheet with field-level HL7 to FHIR mappings

messages.body:
Tom,

Got it, thanks for delivering on time. Linda and I will dig in tomorrow morning.

Marcus

Marcus,

I've been through the mapping. The standard FHIR resources look fine, but I have significant concerns about Section 3 (ADT) and Section 4 (extensions). 

ADT issues:
- The A08 (patient update) segment mapping is wrong for fields PID-18 and PID-19. PID-18 (patient account number) is being mapped to identifier.value but it needs a system discriminator, otherwise we'll get collisions with the MRN. There's a legacy system quirk where PID-19 (SSN) is sometimes populated with a dummy value (999-99-9999) for privacy reasons — this will cause problems in the FHIR Observation if not handled.
- The A03 (discharge) segment is missing the discharge disposition code. That's a regulatory requirement for our billing workflow.

Extension issues:
- The 15 custom lab extensions look mostly right but extension #7 (result_comment_routing_flag) has the wrong cardinality — it should be 0..* not 0..1, because some results can have multiple routing flags.
- Extension #12 (external_lab_source_id) — Tom has this as a string but it should be a Coding type because we need to track which reference lab sent the result, not just a free-text ID.

Also the 3 [NEEDS_CLARIFICATION] fields — I know two of them. I'll write those up for Tom. The third one (PV1-52, the "other healthcare provider" field) I genuinely don't know. It's populated inconsistently in the source system. We may need to loop in the clinical informatics team.

I'll put this all in a comment doc. Can you review before I send to Tom?

Linda

Linda — these are substantive issues, not just nits. Good catch on the discharge disposition — that would've been a billing headache. Let's consolidate into a clean comment log and I'll add context on the PV1-52 field (I'll check with Dr. Okonkwo in clinical informatics). Give me until Monday to pull that together and then we'll send it to Tom as a joint response.

Marcus

Linda — got clarity on PV1-52 from Dr. Okonkwo: we should map this to Encounter.participant with participantType = "PART" when populated, and ignore/drop when null (which is about 60% of records). He confirmed the inconsistent population is by design — it's only filled when there's a consulting provider involvement, not just the attending.

OK I think we have everything. Can you finish the comment doc today so we can get it to Tom on the 20th?

Marcus
